campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and money in the treasury. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at different ways people can get around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasury report and cash management
Multimodal transportation plan - options for walking, biking, transit, and driving

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (a local law) and decided to fast-track it for a final vote at their next meeting instead of going through the normal multi-step approval process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input
New ordinance introduced and expedited for adoption
Ordinance being placed on consent calendar for next meeting vote
First reading waived to speed up approval process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and review the city's financial health for January. The council also approved a major housing development project (Solana) and confirmed plans for community health and senior services throughout the year.
keyTopics: Approved the Solana Project development (Tract 10657)
Reviewed January 2026 financial and investment reports
Confirmed 2026 community health and senior services programs
Updated the General Plan and Housing Element progress

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7th and April 14th, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each meeting, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked on any legal notice requirements. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for April 7, 2026 council meeting
Reviewed draft agenda for April 14, 2026 meeting
Set time limits for specific agenda items to keep meetings on track
Verified legal notification requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's Human Relations Committee meeting scheduled for April 2, 2026 has been canceled. The committee will hold its next meeting on May 7, 2026.
keyTopics: Human Relations Committee meeting canceled
Next meeting scheduled for May 7, 2026
No discussion topics listed for this date

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and approve applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding, which helps pay for housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The commission also approved minutes from their previous February meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking this grant money.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant funding applications reviewed
Housing and community development programs discussed
February 25 meeting minutes approved
Federal funding for local housing initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 6, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 6, 2026, where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The main business included selecting a chair and vice chair for the Legislative Review Committee for 2026, and discussing the upcoming legislative calendar and priorities for the year.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on city matters
Election of Legislative Review Committee leadership for 2026
2026 legislative calendar and priorities
State law limitations on making decisions on non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where they reviewed and filed a report from the City Auditor's Office about how Silicon Valley Power handles its power purchase agreements. The meeting was available both in-person and online so residents could participate from home.
keyTopics: Audit of Silicon Valley Power's power purchase agreement process
City Auditor's Office report reviewed and filed
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom available)
Public participation options via video conference or phone

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss a new program that would help residents get security cameras for their homes through a grant, and reviewed the city's spending plans for the next five years (2027-2031). The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed what items will be discussed at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Home security camera grant program for residents
Five-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031)
Five-year budget and financial planning for the city
Approval of previous meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Utilities Advisory Commission met to review and approve the city's gas utility budget plan for fiscal year 2027, which includes changes to how gas rates are charged for homes and businesses. The commission recommended that the City Council adopt this financial plan, which adjusts the rate schedules for residential customers, multi-unit buildings, and large commercial users.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates increasing for residential customers (Schedule G-1)
Rate changes for apartment buildings and small businesses (Schedule G-2)
Separate rate adjustments for large commercial gas users (Schedule G-3)
Financial reserves and transfers between utility and general city funds
